title: drymocallis glandulosa var. glandulosa
intro: Drymocallis glandulosa var. glandulosa, also known as the glandular twinflower or sticky twinflower, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health issues, including wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Drymocallis glandulosa var. glandulosa have been found to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, contributing to its potential health benefits. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
